title: What to Do When You Don’t Know the Answer
primary_keyword: what to do when you don’t know the answer
secondary_keywords: Christian apologetics when you do not know the answer
how to answer hard questions honestly
what should Christians say when they are stuck
search_intent: A regular Christian, small-group leader, youth leader or student wants to know what to say when someone asks a hard question they cannot answer immediately.
likely_follow_up_questions: Is it bad if a Christian does not know the answer?
What should I say when I am stuck?
Does apologetics mean I need to answer everything instantly?
How can a small group practise honest answers?
natural_app_conversion_path: Invite Christians to practise difficult questions in Answerable before real conversations, especially when they need a patient and honest way to respond.
strengthens_existing_cluster_or_starts_n: Strengthens the conversation tactics and gracious posture cluster without reusing a doctrinal question card or duplicating an existing core answer.

title: Bible Reliability Questions: How to Answer Without Sounding Defensive
secondary_keywords: how to answer Bible reliability questions
search_intent: A Christian, student, small-group leader, or seeker wants a calm way to answer Bible reliability questions about manuscripts and variants without sounding defensive.
likely_follow_up_questions: Do variants mean the New Testament was corrupted?
Which New Testament variants matter most?
How should I answer a friend who says the Bible changed over time?
How can a small group practise Bible reliability questions?
natural_app_conversion_path: Invite Christians and group leaders to practise Bible reliability answers in Answerable before real conversations, focusing on clear claims and gracious tone.
strengthens_existing_cluster_or_starts_n: Strengthens the Bible reliability cluster by linking the specific scribal-copying article to a broader reader-intent article about answering Bible reliability questions calmly.

title: Is the Trinity a contradiction?
secondary_keywords: how can God be one and three
search_intent: A Christian, seeker, or sceptic wants to know whether the Trinity is logically contradictory.

title: Did scribes change the New Testament?
primary_keyword: did scribes change the New Testament
search_intent: A Christian, student, or sceptic wants to know whether copying errors destroyed confidence in the New Testament text.

title: Could the disciples have hallucinated Jesus?
primary_keyword: could the disciples have hallucinated Jesus
search_intent: A reader wants to know whether grief or group hallucination explains the resurrection testimony.

title: How should Christians answer slavery in the Bible?
primary_keyword: slavery in the Bible Christian answer
secondary_keywords: how to answer slavery in the Bible
search_intent: A Christian or sceptic wants a morally clear and careful answer to slavery texts in the Bible.

title: How can a small group practise apologetics without arguing?
secondary_keywords: how to practise apologetics without arguing
search_intent: A pastor, small-group leader, youth leader, or regular Christian wants a practical way to practise hard apologetics questions without turning the group combative.
source_basis: tone training from question cards g02, b08 and r03
likely_follow_up_questions: Which apologetics question should a small group start with?
How do we keep apologetics training gentle?
How can youth leaders practise hard questions safely?
natural_app_conversion_path: Invite group leaders and Christians to practise model answers and Scripture-led responses in the Answerable app before real conversations.
strengthens_existing_cluster_or_starts_n: Starts a church training and gracious posture cluster while linking existing doctrine, Bible and resurrection posts.
